prediction_reason.历年位次与招生计划: 2021–2025年最低位次分别为约36296→25276→24430→24862→27005名，整体呈先升后降再小幅后移的波动趋势；2025年基准位次约27005名，较2024年后移约2143名 ，近两年呈后移态势；该校在浙江省综合类招生计划2021年67人、2022年68人、2023年79人、2024年93人、2025年83人、2026年95人；2026年较2025年扩 招12人（+14.5%），扩招幅度较大，理论上使竞争略有缓解，但位次数字仍受其他因素综合影响。
prediction_reason.学校层次与声誉: 华中师范大学为211工程、双一流建设高校；软科中国大学排 名2021年第46→2022年第50→2023年第47→2024年第58→2025年第56→2026年第53名，近年排名稳定在50名左右，2026年小幅回升至第53名，学校综合实力稳中有升；2026年高考周期声誉评价为偏正面，主要涉及科研成果或重大荣誉（81岁博士在华师毕业等正面事件）；抖音热度排名第89位，评价为一般，综合热度约21.6万，短视频平台曝 光度处于中等偏下水平，未出现明显负面舆情或投档断档记录；第五轮学科评估中，教育学获评A+（全国顶尖），政治学、心理学、中国史、马克思主义理论获 评A-，共5个A类学科；教育学A+为师范类院校最高等级，对报考师范类专业的考生有较强吸引力。
prediction_reason.专业热度与地域: 师范/教育类专业社会热度2024年为分化观望（heat_in dex 55.84），整体平稳偏推荐；计算机类、电子信息/通信类专业持续高热推荐，与该校开设的计算机类、电子信息类等热门工科专业方向吻合；浙江省考生总数2021年25.3万→2025 年29.1万→2026年约40.0万（普通高考总人数），增幅约37.6%，竞争显著加剧；华中师范大学位于湖北武汉，在浙江省无 本地招生倾斜；该校2025年全国招生总人数中浙江省占比约1.5%（9人/598人），属外省招生占比较低的学校，受地域偏好影响较小。
prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合2025年基准位次27005 名、2026年扩招14.5%、浙江省考生总数大幅增加约37.6%导致竞争加剧、声誉偏正面吸引更多优质生源、软科排名小幅回升等因素，预计2026年最 低录取位次；预测最低位次约29548名，区间29048–30048名。

major_tier_recommendation.tier_1:
一档（最难进）
- 汉语言文学（国家拔尖学生培养基地班）
- 中国语言文学类
- 马克思主义理论类
- 政治学类
- 数学类
- 法学类。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_2:
二档（中等）
- 教育学类（含教育学、融合教育等）
- 心理学类
- 新闻传播学类
- 物理学类
- 统计学
- 社会学类。
major_tier_recommendation.tier_3:
三档（相对好进）
- 化学类
- 计算机类（含计算机科学与技术、软件工程等）
- 生物科学类
- 电子信息类
- 管理科学与工程类
- 金融学类。
major_tier_recommendation.overall_advice: 位次在预测区间29048–30048名内，建议以二档专业为主攻方向（教育学A+、心理学A-等优势学科），一档专业（汉语言文学基地班、马克思主义理论、政治学等）作为冲刺，三 档专业（计算机类、电子信息类等热门工科及化学类、生物科学类）作为稳妥保底。位次明显优于区间下限（29048名以上）时可重点冲刺一档王牌专业；位次接近区间上限（300 48名）时建议以三档中社会热度较高的计算机类、电子信息类为主。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plan
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings were approximately 36296 → 25276 → 24430 → 24862 → 27005, showing an overall trend of first rising, then falling, and then slightly declining; the baseline ranking for 2025 is about 27005, which is about 2143 places lower than 2024, indicating a downward trend in recent two years; the comprehensive enrollment plan for this university in Zhejiang Province was 67 in 2021, 68 in 2022, 79 in 2023, 93 in 2024, 83 in 2025, and 95 in 2026; compared to 2025, the enrollment in 2026 expands by 12 students (+14.5%), a relatively large expansion, theoretically easing competition slightly, but the ranking numbers are still affected by other factors.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Central China Normal University is a 211 Project and Double First-Class university; its Soft Science University Ranking was 46th in 2021 → 50th in 2022 → 47th in 2023 → 58th in 2024 → 56th in 2025 → 53rd in 2026, with rankings stable around 50th in recent years and a slight rebound to 53rd in 2026, indicating a steady improvement in overall strength; the reputation evaluation for the 2026 college entrance examination cycle is slightly positive, mainly involving scientific research achievements or major honors (e.g., an 81-year-old doctoral graduate from Central China Normal University, etc.); its Douyin popularity ranking is 89th, rated as average, with a comprehensive popularity of about 216,000, indicating medium-low exposure on short video platforms, with no obvious negative public opinion or enrollment gaps; in the fifth round of discipline evaluation, Education was rated A+ (top in the country), Political Science, Psychology, Chinese History, and Marxist Theory were rated A-, totaling 5 A-level disciplines; Education A+ is the highest level among normal universities, strongly attracting candidates applying for teacher-training majors.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: The social popularity of teacher-training/education majors in 2024 was divided and wait-and-see (heat index 55.84), overall stable with a slight recommendation; computer, electronic information/communication majors continue to be highly popular and recommended, aligning with the university's popular engineering majors such as computer and electronic information; the total number of candidates in Zhejiang Province was 253,000 in 2021 → 291,000 in 2025 → about 400,000 in 2026 (total number of regular college entrance exam takers), an increase of about 37.6%, significantly intensifying competition; Central China Normal University is located in Wuhan, Hubei, with no local enrollment preference in Zhejiang Province; in 2025, the proportion of Zhejiang candidates in the university's total national enrollment was about 1.5% (9 out of 598), indicating a relatively low proportion of out-of-province enrollment, with little impact from regional preferences.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the baseline ranking of 27005 in 2025, a 14.5% enrollment expansion in 2026, a significant increase of about 37.6% in the total number of candidates in Zhejiang Province leading to intensified competition, a slightly positive reputation attracting more high-quality students, and a slight rebound in Soft Science ranking, the predicted minimum admission ranking for 2026 is approximately 29548, with a range of 29048–30048.

major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_1:
Tier 1 (Most Difficult to Enter)
- Chinese Language and Literature (National Top Talent Cultivation Base Class)
- Chinese Language and Literature Category
- Marxist Theory Category
- Political Science Category
- Mathematics Category
- Law Category.
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_2:
Tier 2 (Moderate)
- Education Category (including Education, Integrated Education, etc.)
- Psychology Category
- Journalism and Communication Category
- Physics Category
- Statistics
- Sociology Category.
major_tier_recommendation_en.tier_3:
Tier 3 (Relatively Easier to Enter)
- Chemistry Category
- Computer Category (including Computer Science and Technology, Software Engineering, etc.)
- Biological Sciences Category
- Electronic Information Category
- Management Science and Engineering Category
- Finance Category.
major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: For rankings within the predicted range of 29048–30048, it is recommended to focus on Tier 2 majors (Education A+, Psychology A-, and other advantageous disciplines) as the main direction, Tier 1 majors (Chinese Language and Literature Base Class, Marxist Theory, Political Science, etc.) as a sprint, and Tier 3 majors (popular engineering fields like Computer and Electronic Information, as well as Chemistry and Biological Sciences) as a safe backup. When the ranking is significantly better than the lower limit of the range (above 29048), prioritize sprinting for Tier 1 flagship majors; when the ranking is close to the upper limit of the range (30048), it is recommended to focus on Tier 3 majors with high social popularity, such as Computer and Electronic Information.
